evals.name: commit-trigger
evals.prompt: I just pushed a commit: `feat(auth): add JWT token validation middleware`. The middleware checks tokens on protected routes and returns 401 if invalid. We chose JWT over session cookies because the API needs to be stateless.
evals.expected_output: Triage runs first, classifies JWT middleware and decision as new items, capture creates 2 linked docs (component + decision) with 401 behavior and stateless rationale folded in rather than as separate nodes.

assertions.description: Workflow trace explicitly explains which items were folded and why
401 behavior and stateless rationale are NOT separate nodes — folded into parent docs
Exactly 2 docs created (component + decision), not 3 or 4
Both docs link to each other with [[wikilinks]]

evals.name: convention-decision
evals.prompt: We just decided that all API endpoints will use snake_case for JSON field names going forward. This is the new convention across the whole backend. No more camelCase in API responses.
evals.expected_output: Triage runs first, classifies as new convention item, capture creates a single convention doc. Skip-sync rule explicitly cited. Granularity reasoning documented.

assertions.description: Trace explains why the convention gets its own node
The explicit skip-sync rule is cited when sync is skipped
Exactly 1 doc created
Created doc has type: convention in frontmatter

evals.name: refactor-existing
evals.prompt: Just committed a refactor: `refactor(hooks): move rate limiting from Express middleware to a dedicated RateLimiter service class`. The old middleware approach is removed. Existing docs might reference the old middleware.
evals.expected_output: Triage finds no existing docs, pivots to capture. Skip-sync rule cited explicitly. Middleware removal detail folded into component node, not a separate ADR stub.

assertions.description: Trace explains which items get own nodes vs which are folded
The explicit skip-sync rule is cited when triage finds no existing docs
The 'old middleware removal' detail is folded into the component node, not a separate ADR stub
At least 1 doc is created (triage→capture pivot for new component)

evals.name: no-trigger-knowledge-question
evals.prompt: Why did we decide to avoid session cookies in the API? I thought sessions were simpler to implement.
evals.expected_output: Workflow does NOT run — this is a knowledge question, not an action. explore-knowledge handles it directly via its own description. autology-workflow should not intercept Q&A.

assertions.description: autology-workflow does not invoke triage, capture, or sync — knowledge questions are not actions
No docs/ node is created
The question is answered (by explore-knowledge or directly) without routing through autology-workflow

evals.name: sync-only
evals.prompt: Just pushed a fix: `fix(rate-limiter): switch from stdout logging to structured logger`. The RateLimiter service already has docs. Only the logging behavior changed — no architectural decision, no new component.
evals.expected_output: Triage finds existing RateLimiter docs (→ sync). No new items to capture. Sync runs alone. Skip-capture rule cited explicitly.

assertions.description: Triage classifies the RateLimiter change as Existing (→ sync), not New
sync-knowledge runs to update the existing doc
The rule 'if triage returns only existing items, skip capture' is cited explicitly
No new docs/ node is created

evals.name: mixed-batch
evals.prompt: Pushed `feat(storage): add S3FileStorage service for file uploads`. Also updated the existing FileService to delegate large file uploads to S3FileStorage. FileService already has a doc.
evals.expected_output: Triage classifies S3FileStorage as new (→ capture) and FileService as existing (→ sync). Both capture and sync run. Capture creates S3FileStorage node; sync updates FileService doc.

assertions.description: Both capture-knowledge and sync-knowledge run (mixed triage result)
A new doc is created for S3FileStorage
FileService doc is updated via sync (not a new node)
Triage output clearly separates Existing and New sections

evals.name: mid-session-decision
evals.prompt: Actually, let's settle this now: all internal service-to-service calls will use gRPC, not REST. That's the standard going forward across all backend services.
evals.expected_output: Workflow captures the decision directly. A convention or decision node is created without waiting for a commit. Triage may run briefly but finds no existing docs on this.

assertions.description: A doc is created for the gRPC decision without requiring a commit
Created doc has type: decision or type: convention in frontmatter
Workflow does not say 'wait for a commit' — captures the decision immediately from conversation

evals.name: convention-in-review
evals.prompt: I noticed while reviewing PRs that we're inconsistent about error responses — some services return `{error: string}`, others return `{code, message, details}`. Let's standardize: all error responses must use `{code, message, details}` going forward.
evals.expected_output: Workflow recognizes this as a new convention established mid-conversation (not a commit). Triage finds no existing docs. Capture creates a convention node.

assertions.description: A convention doc is created for the error response standard
Created doc has type: convention in frontmatter
Doc body describes both the old inconsistency and the new standard

evals.name: no-trigger-coding-task
evals.prompt: Can you help me add pagination to the /users endpoint? I want cursor-based pagination using the user's created_at timestamp.
evals.expected_output: Workflow does NOT run. Claude helps implement the pagination without invoking triage, capture, or sync. No docs are created.

assertions.description: No autology sub-skill (triage, capture, sync, explore) is invoked
No docs/ node is created — implementation help only
Claude responds with pagination implementation help, not knowledge management

evals.name: no-trigger-brainstorm
evals.prompt: I'm trying to decide whether to use WebSockets or Server-Sent Events for real-time notifications. What are the tradeoffs? We haven't decided yet.
evals.expected_output: Workflow does NOT run — this is an open question with no decision made. Claude discusses tradeoffs. No docs created. Capture should only fire when a decision is actually made.

assertions.description: No triage or capture invoked — no decision has been made yet
No docs/ node is created for an undecided question
Claude discusses WebSocket vs SSE tradeoffs without treating it as a finalized decision

evals.name: no-trigger-debugging
evals.prompt: My auth middleware is throwing 401 for valid tokens. Here's the error: `JsonWebTokenError: invalid signature`. The tokens are generated by a different service. Help me debug.
evals.expected_output: Workflow does NOT run. Claude helps debug the JWT signature mismatch. No docs created — a bug investigation is not a decision or convention.

assertions.description: No autology sub-skill invoked — debugging is not knowledge capture
No docs/ node created during debugging
Claude investigates the JWT signature issue rather than routing to knowledge management

evals.name: no-trigger-wip
evals.prompt: I'm designing the token refresh flow. The idea is to use a sliding window — each API call extends the session by 15 minutes. Can you help me think through edge cases like concurrent requests and clock skew?
evals.expected_output: Workflow does NOT run — this is a design discussion, not a finalized decision. Claude helps think through edge cases. Nothing is captured until a decision is confirmed.

assertions.description: No triage or capture invoked — design is in progress, not finalized
No docs/ node created for a work-in-progress design
Claude engages with edge cases (concurrent requests, clock skew) rather than capturing an undecided design
